LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND GREG SUHR'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - CCSF'S DEMURRER TO THE RETALIATION CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND PLAINTIFF DID NOT NEED TO EXHAUST HER ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. SEE LLOYD V. COUNTY OF LA (2009) 172 CAL.APP.4TH 320. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND ON THE RETALIATION CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO CHIEF SUHR BECAUSE HE WAS NOT PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYER. THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER TO THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ADVANTAGE IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS REGARDING AN ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE PLAINTIFF AND SOME THIRD PARTY, WITH THE PROBABILITY OF FUTURE ECONOMIC BENEFIT TO THE PLAINTIFF. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. THE ALLEGATIONS DO NOT CLEARLY IMPLICATE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION FROM CHIEF SUHR'S PERSONNEL FILE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (Redacted)
Off Calendar Mar-19-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RANDALL E. STRAUSS OF THE GWILLIAM, IVARY & BREWER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (510) 832-5411 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JONATHAN C. ROLNICK, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3815 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND GREG SUHR'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues - The motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. There are triable issues of material regarding whether plaintiff can establish the retaliation cause of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff's disclosure can support the claim. See Hager v. County of Los Angeles (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 1538. The court finds Hager to be better reasoned than Mize-Kurzman v. Marin Community College Dist. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 832, and relies on Hager. See McCallum v. McCallum (1987) 190 Cal.App.3d 308, 315, fn. 4. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff reasonably believed that Chief Suhr was violating the Penal Code. There are triable issues of material fact regarding whether there is a causal link between the protected conduct and the adverse employment action and whether the reasons for the termination were pretextual. The trier of fact could find that the timing of the termination decision was suspicious. Chief Suhr's counsel threatened plaintiff (Plaintiff's Dec., par. 10-11). Chief Suhr admitted knowledge of the investigation and plaintiff's role. (Suhr depo., 6:10-7:8; 8:6-9:6). Lastly, the declaration of Mr. Tidwell undermines Chief Suhr's proffered reasons for the adverse employment action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff properly filed a government claim with defendant and defendant received it. First, plaintiff declares that she mailed the claim (Plaintiff's Dec., par. 26). Gov't Code sec. 915.2 provides that presentation is made at that time. Second, Sgt. Goss declares that it was Police Legal's practice to forward the claims to the City Attorney's Office, which is in the same building as the Controller. (Goss Dec., par. 9). There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff can establish the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. The court construes the claim as a statutory claim per Gov't 815.2(a) [public employer liable for acts or omissions of its employees] and 820(a) [public employee liable to same extent as private person]. Worker's compensation does not bar the claim because retaliation that violates California's public policy is outside the employment bargain. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/ehg

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAYME L. BURNS OF GWILLIAM, IVARY & BREWER, TEL. NO. (510) 832-5411 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JONATHAN C. ROLNICK, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3815 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND GREG SUHR'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint - CCSF's demurrer to the retaliation cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges protected activity and plaintiff did not need to exhaust her administrative remedies. See Lloyd v. County of LA (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 320. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend on the retaliation cause of action as to Chief Suhr because he was not plaintiff's employer. The Court overrules the demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. The demurrer to the cause of action for intentional interference with prospective advantage is sustained with leave to amend to plead facts regarding an economic relationship between the plaintiff and some third party, with the probability of future economic benefit to the plaintiff. The motion to strike is denied. The allegations do not clearly implicate confidential information from Chief Suhr's personnel file. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
